title: iphigenia caricina
intro: Iphigenia caricina, also known as the marsh marigold, is a perennial plant native to wetlands in Europe, Asia, and North America. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Iphigenia caricina include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and skin conditions like eczema. Herbal preparations of Iphigenia caricina include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
